_README: Externalized model bundle config for scripts/pseudolabel.py.
This file is the ONLY place the (unsettled) model identity + label
space lives, so re-training (e.g. normal-spine-only, or un-merging
L5/L6) needs NO code change — just edit this file.
One entry per region model. pseudolabel.py fills the MISSING region of
spine_only / pelvic_native (separate-mode) records using the model whose
fills_region matches, and NEVER overwrites a manual voxel.
label_remap: model-output integer class -> canonical 10-class id
(background 0 and any class absent from the map are dropped). This is
where you encode e.g. last_lumbar -> {5,6} decisions; if the model
cannot separate L5/L6 you map its merged class to a single canonical
id and accept that limitation (documented per-model in 'notes').
supplies_canonical: canonical ids this model is TRUSTED to write. A
predicted class remapped outside this set is discarded (defensive).
set enabled=true only once the checkpoints for that model are final.

models.name: spine
models.notes: FILL ME. Current checkpoints were trained on LSTV cases with L5+L6 merged into one 'last_lumbar' class — decide the remap (or retrain on normal spines) before enabling.

models.name: pelvis
models.notes: FILL ME. Only needed to complete spine_only scans (pelvis missing). If you have no pelvis model, leave disabled — those records are skipped, not fabricated.
